'''Female''' is one of the four [[grammatical genders]] in [[Aelaki]]. It corresponds to the early reproductive life stage of the speaker's species, which are sequential hermaphrodites progressing from [[Child|child]] to female to [[Male|male]].

== Overview ==

The female gender is used for animate nouns referring to beings in their early reproductive stage. For invertebrates and artefacts that lack a clear biological life stage, gender may be assigned by perceived age or sound symbolism.

== Vowel pattern ==

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Number !! Vowel
|-
| Singular || '''o'''
|-
| Collective || '''e'''
|}

== Ki syllable table ==

The following table shows the [[Ki syllable|ki syllables]] for female gender across all four persons, in singular, collective, and [[Inanimate|inanimate]]-marked forms:

{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Person !! Singular !! Reduplication !! Collective !! Inanimate-marked
|-
| 1st person || tho || thotho || the || thof
|-
| 2nd person || jo || jojo || je || jof
|-
| 3rd person || sho || shosho || she || shof
|-
| 4th person || ko || koko || ke || kof
|}

== Relationship to other genders ==

As [[Aelaki]] speakers mature, they transition biologically from [[Child|child]] to female to [[Male|male]]. Nouns tracking a living being normally shift gender to match the being's current life stage. The [[Inanimate|inanimate]] gender stands apart, reserved for non-living and mass nouns.

== See also ==

* [[Child]]
* [[Male]]
* [[Inanimate]]

[[Category:grammatical genders]]
